data.current_description: Update the value of a datastream by pin or datastream ID
data.description_suggestion: Update the value of a datastream by pin or datastream ID. Accepts the new value as input and modifies the datastream state on the Blynk server. Returns confirmation of the update. This operation persists the new value and may trigger associated device actions or notifications.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate, but description lacks critical details. data.current_description: Update multiple datastream values in a single request
data.description_suggestion: Update multiple datastream values in a single request for improved efficiency. Accepts a batch payload containing multiple datastream updates (by pin or datastream ID with new values). Returns confirmation of all updates processed. All updates are applied atomically to the device state on the Blynk server.

data.current_description: Trigger a log event for the device
data.description_suggestion: Log a custom event for the device to the Blynk event history. Accepts event name and optional event data as input. Creates a timestamped event record associated with the device that can be retrieved via device history queries. Returns confirmation of event creation. Useful for tracking device state changes, alerts, or custom application events.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about what 'log event' means in the Blynk context. Missing details about input parameters (event name, data), output confirmation, and the purpose/use case. data.current_description: Retrieve historical data for a device datastream
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve historical data for a device datastream within a specified time range. Accepts datastream identifier (pin or datastream ID) and time range parameters as input.
